notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A break below the $260.22 52w low on rising volume, or a fresh negative company-specific catalyst (guidance cut, program loss), would confirm idiosyncratic breakdown and flip me to a positive/trim tactical.

positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print below 0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or EPS revisions turning down >1% over 90d, would confirm backlog/earnings erosion and flip this to a trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that op margin is inflecting back toward mid-teens (guidance raise or two consecutive quarters of margin expansion) alongside forward P/E falling to a discount vs cohort would flip this to a buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp non-fundamental single-day drop of >5% (e.g., a China export-control headline) would flip this to a tactical buy; a blow-off spike RSI >75 would flip it to a trim.

positional.falsifier: A break below the 50-day ($1722) that fails to reclaim, or a >15% drawdown from highs, would flip positional to a stronger buy on the pullback (or confirm trend damage).

strategic.falsifier: A book-to-bill sustained above 1.0 with rising backlog AND accelerating TSMC capex (plus forward P/E compressing toward <25x) would flip this negative (buy); conversely a China revenue collapse + multiple leading-edge customers cutting capex would push it toward strong trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp risk-on rotation (VIX collapses below 14, cyclicals/high-beta lead) draining the quality bid would flip this positive as LIN lags a rallying tape.

positional.falsifier: A decisive break below the 200-day SMA ($472) on backlog deceleration or a widening peer discount would flip this positive.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmed like-for-like price/mix ex-FX turning negative alongside backlog YoY going negative would validate genuine moat erosion and push the score more positive; conversely a >$1B H2 project win or backlog acceleration flips it negative.

tactical.falsifier: MSFT reclaiming the 50DMA ($401) on rising volume with RSI pushing >65 would flip this from a buy-the-dip to a chase-strength setup (score toward zero/positive); conversely a decisive break below the $349 52w low would confirm knife-catching risk.

positional.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth re-accelerating above 30% on the next print (broad-based, AI-driven) would validate the buy and could push the score more negative; conversely Azure decelerating below 22% with capex still climbing would flip this to a trim as the quality-compounder thesis would be impaired.

strategic.falsifier: An unfavorable OpenAI partnership renegotiation (loss of exclusivity, adverse AGI/IP clause) or Azure CC decel below 22% would impair the strategic moat and flip this positive; conversely confirmed Azure re-acceleration + Copilot inflection would push more negative.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp 10Y move (>25bps in either direction) in a single session that creates a sympathetic overreaction in ENB, or RSI spiking >70 on a volume blowoff.

positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps (either via a 10Y drop below ~3.4% or an ENB selloff), or a rate-cut cycle beginning — either would flip this to a buy.

strategic.falsifier: Signs of a sustained BoC/Fed easing cycle (real yields falling toward ~1.5%) or a major accretive contracted growth catalyst (LNG Canada Phase 2 FID) that lifts long-term EBITDA guidance — either flips strategic to buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close above the $14.87 50-day SMA on strong volume would flip this to a buy-side tactical read.

positional.falsifier: A break below the 200-day SMA ($12.78) combined with HY OAS widening past ~3.5bps would flip this to a trim.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the copper cycle is peaking rather than troughing — trailing P/E compressing toward single digits alongside falling LME copper — would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sustained break and hold below the $32.84 52w low on rising volume would flip this to a positive (trim) tactical score; conversely a decisive reclaim of the 50DMA ($38.47) with expanding volume would push it more negative.

positional.falsifier: Sustained ETF outflows plus break of $32.84 low flips positive.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the halving cycle has decisively broken (e.g., sustained new highs with strong flows well beyond typical cycle-top timing) would flip this to negative; a confirmed cyclical top with collapsing on-chain activity would push it clearly positive.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp MXN move (>2% in a day) or a decisive break of the 200-day SMA with volume would flip the tactical sign.

positional.falsifier: A sustained MXN depreciation past ~18.5 or a volume/margin miss in the next quarterly report would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: A Banxico dovish pivot that drives MXN structurally weaker (toward 19-20) or a US tariff regime on Mexico would flip this to positive.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp move above the 50-day SMA ($18.15) on volume with RSI >65 would flip this to a sell/trim tactical read; a break below the 200-day ($15.96) would flip it to a buy.

positional.falsifier: WTI breaking below $70 or a concrete government intervention on fuel pricing/dividends would flip this positive (trim); a special dividend confirmation would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A government mandate forcing below-market fuel pricing or large forced non-core capex (flipping to trim), OR a credible multi-year capital-return framework with governance protections (deepening the buy).

tactical.falsifier: A sustained 2-3 week move in Drewry WCI (up >15% would flip me negative/buy; down >15% breaking $12.31 would flip me positive/trim).

positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0.5 alongside WCI normalizing would confirm the trim thesis (score more positive); GSCPI pushing above 2.0 with rising spot rates would flip me to buy (negative).

strategic.falsifier: Logistics segment margins turning sustainably negative or a debt-funded acquisition breaking the balance-sheet-quality thesis would flip me to positive/trim; conversely a scrapping-driven capacity discipline announcement would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the SMA50 ($485) on rising volume, or a break of the recent uptrend after a failed 200-day test, would flip this positive (trim).

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling back below +10% (funding thaw stalling) plus a break below SMA50, or peer commentary (DHR/Sartorius) turning cautious again, would flip this toward trim.

strategic.falsifier: Q1/Q2 2026 organic growth failing to inflect above ~2-3% AND peer commentary (DHR, Sartorius, Repligen) signaling renewed destocking would flip the cycle-bottoming thesis and turn this to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close back above the 50-day ($103.54) on expanding volume with IGV outperforming SPY would flip this to a confirmed reversal (more negative); conversely a break below the 52w low $81.24 confirms the downtrend and flips toward positive/trim.

positional.falsifier: A golden-cross reclaim (50-day crossing back above 200-day) or two consecutive quarters of positive EPS revisions would confirm the turn (more negative); continued negative revisions plus a break of $81.24 flips to positive.

strategic.falsifier: An earnings print showing cRPO re-accelerating (>20% YoY) with Now Assist ASP inflection flips this decisively negative (buy); confirmation of cRPO decel below 18% or a federal contract loss flips it firmly positive (trim).

tactical.falsifier: A break back below the 200-day SMA (~$529) on rising volume, or RSI rolling under 50, would flip this from mild-trim to a genuine buy-the-dip setup.

positional.falsifier: Cross-border volume growth printing <8% or two consecutive quarters of negative EPS revisions would flip the positional score positive.

strategic.falsifier: Measurable real-economy merchant stablecoin displacement (a major retailer routing meaningful POS volume off card rails) OR interchange/network-fee legislation advancing to a floor vote would flip this strategic score to positive.

tactical.falsifier: The Q2 print (or its immediate reaction) showing procedures BELOW 12% paired with explicit US procedure-share loss to Hugo, or transition quantified as material — that flips a fear-buy into a genuine-erosion sell.

positional.falsifier: Post-print EPS revisions turning sharply negative (90d consensus cut >10%) or I&A growth decoupling below procedure growth — confirmation the 2027 transition is arriving early and the drawdown is fundamentally justified, not sentiment.

strategic.falsifier: Management quantifying the 2027 instrument transition as MATERIAL, or the first quarter of measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo — either flips moat_status to eroding/transition to quantified_material and turns this into a structural sell.
